questions.body: In Mendel's experiments with Garden Pea, round seeds shape (RR) were dominant over wrinkled seeds (rr), yellow cotyledons (YY) were dominant over green cotyledons (yy). What are expected phenotypes in F2 generation RRYY x rryy?

choices.b: only wrinkled seeds with yellow cotyledons
choices.c: only round seeds with green cotyledons
choices.d: round seeds with yellow cotyledons and wrinkled seeds with cotyledons

questions.body: A colour blind man and a phenotypically normal woman have four children (each has 46 chromosomes)-a boy with haemophilia but no other traits; a boy with color blindness but no other trails; a color-blind girls but no other traits; and a phenotypically normal girl (all traits mentioned are inherited as X-linked recessives). What is the probability that the normal is heterpzygous for color blindness?

questions.body:

List-I (Character of man): (P) Sex-linked, (Q) Sex-Influenced, (R) Sex-limited
List-II (Example): 1. Baldness, 2. Acquired immune deficiency syndrome, 3. Klinefelter's syndrome, 4. Haemophilia, 5. Tuft of hairs (hypertrichosis) on pinna

questions.body: Male child with blood group AB is colourblind. His parents could be

choices.a: father normal vision with blood group A, mother colourblind with group O
choices.b: father colour blind with group O, mother colour blind with blood group AB
choices.c: father normal vision with blood group A, mother colourblind with blood group B
choices.d: father colourblind with blood group O, mother normal vision with blood group O

questions.body: Albinism is a condition that results from the lack of normal pigmentation. Individuals possessing two recessive alleles (aa) are albino. Similarly, attached earlobes result from two recessive alleles (ee). Ina family, the husband is an albino with attached earlobes. What is the genotype of the mother?
questions.subject: biology

questions.body: A species of plant is discovered in which individual plants produce either white or purple flowers. True breeding plants that have short stems with flowers are crossed with true breeding plants that have tall stems and purple flowers. The resulting offspring all have tall with purple flowers. When one of these tall, purple-flowered offspring is crossed with a short, white-floweredplant, plants with the following characteristics were obtained in equal proportions. White flowers on tall stems and purple flowers on tall stems. White flowers on short stems, purple flowers on short stems. Given the information above, which of the following is most likely true?

choices.a: the white-flower allele and is most likely true?
choices.b: the genes for flower colour and stem length are closely linked
choices.c: extensive closing-over between the purple-flower allele and the white allele has occurred
choices.d: the genes for flower color and stem length are not linked

questions.subject: biology

questions.body:
A male fruit fly (Drosophila melanogaster) with red eyes and long wings was mated with a female with purple eyes and vestigial wings. All of the offspring in the F1 generation red eyes and long wings. These F1 flies were test crossed with purple-eyed, vestigial-winged flies. Their offspring, the F2 generation, appeared as indicated below.
F2
125 red eyes, long wings
124 purple eyes, vestigial wings
18 purple eyes, long wings
16 red eyes, vestigial wings
283 Total
If on the F1 and F2 generation the same characteristics appeared in both male and females, it would be safe to assume that these traits for eye color and wing length...

choices.b: vary in dominance according to sex
choices.d: follow the Mendelian rule of independent assortment

questions.subject: biology

questions.body:
A retrovirus is an RNA virus with the ability to convert its sequence into DNA by reverse transcription. Reverse transcription is mediated by enzyme reverse transcriptase. Which of the following are the functions of reverse transcriptase in retroviruses?
1. it hydrolyzes the host cell's DNA
2. it uses viral RNA as a template for DNA synthesis.
3. it converts host cell RNA into viral DNA.
4. it translates viral RNA into proteins.
5. it uses viral RNA as a template for making complementary RNA strands.
6. it degrades RNA from DNA-RNA hybrid.
questions.subject: biology
